attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Move two sets of keys from the dresser to the white table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided domain file and instruction are mismatched with the visual content of the images. The domain defines actions and predicates for interacting with a microwave, fridge, sink, knife, and receptacles, and the instruction involves moving "keys" from a "dresser" to a "white table". However, the images do not contain any objects that can be identified as keys, a dresser, a microwave, a fridge, a sink, or a knife.

Instead, the images depict a room with two distinct tables:
1.  A white table with a patterned surface, upon which rests a digital alarm clock (displaying "8:25"), a smartphone, two yellow pencils, and a CD. This table has wooden legs.
2.  A wooden table with a checkerboard and a smartphone on its surface. A small blue card is also visible on this table. This table is positioned near a window with green curtains.

The domain's defined types (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`) are not visually represented. The instruction's objects (`keys`, `dresser`) are also absent. The only objects that could potentially be categorized under the domain's `object` type are the smartphones, the alarm clock, the pencils, the CD, and the checkerboard, but none of these are "keys", and neither table is explicitly a "dresser".

Therefore, based on the provided domain and instruction, a scene description that accurately reflects the domain's predicates and the task's requirements cannot be generated from the given images, as the necessary objects and receptacles do not exist in the scene. The scene contains tables, electronic devices, and small items, but none that match the specified task or domain definitions.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
The provided domain file and instruction are fundamentally mismatched with the visual content of the images. The domain defines a set of actions and predicates for interacting with specific kitchen appliances (microwave, fridge, sink) and a knife, along with the concept of "receptacles". The instruction requires moving "keys" from a "dresser" to a "white table".

However, a careful analysis of the images reveals:
- There are two tables: a white table with a patterned top and a wooden table.
- The objects present are: a digital alarm clock, two smartphones, two pencils, a CD, a checkerboard, and a small blue card.
- There are no objects that can be identified as "keys".
- Neither table is a "dresser" in the conventional sense, nor are they defined as "receptacles" in the domain file's context (which implies appliances like fridges or microwaves).
- The domain's core actions (CleanObject, HeatObject, CoolObject, SliceObject) are entirely inapplicable to the objects in the scene.

Therefore, it is impossible to generate a valid PDDL problem file that adheres to the given domain and instruction while accurately reflecting the objects and state in the images. The required objects for the task ("keys", "dresser") do not exist, and the domain's predicates and actions cannot be meaningfully applied to the objects that are present.

The request cannot be fulfilled as specified.

attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation keys1 dresser: keys1 is not a agent
  atLocation keys2 dresser: keys2 is not a agent

        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Move two sets of keys from the dresser to the white table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains two primary surfaces: a white table and a wooden dresser, both positioned near a window with green curtains. The floor is covered with a green carpet, and sunlight casts patterned shadows across the surfaces.

1. Objects and their types:
   - The white table and the wooden dresser are both considered receptacles since they can hold objects on their surfaces.
   - There are two sets of keys visible in the scene. One set is on the wooden dresser, and the other is also on the dresser but slightly offset. These keys are small, flat, rectangular objects with a blue color and a visible keyring or tag.
   - A smartphone is present on both the white table and the dresser, but it is not relevant to the current instruction.
   - A chessboard is on the dresser, but it is not relevant to the task.
   - An alarm clock is on the white table, displaying “8:25”, but it is not relevant to the task.
   - Two yellow pencils and a CD are on the white table, but they are not relevant to the task.
   - The robot (agent) is not visible in any image and is initially not at any object’s location.

2. Quantities:
   - Two sets of keys are present.
   - One white table (receptacle).
   - One wooden dresser (receptacle).

3. Spatial relationships:
   - Both sets of keys are currently located on the wooden dresser.
   - The white table is positioned to the left or adjacent to the dresser (based on image perspective).
   - The robot (agent) is not currently at the location of either the dresser or the white table.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the task:
   - The two sets of keys are not being held by the agent.
   - The keys are not inside any receptacle; they are resting on the surface of the dresser.
   - The white table is currently empty of keys (the keys need to be moved there).
   - The dresser is not openable or closed — it is a static surface, so predicates like (opened ?r) or (openable ?r) do not apply to it as a receptacle in this context.

5. Visual properties:
   - The keys are blue with white text or markings, and they appear to be plastic keycards or fobs rather than traditional metal keys.
   - The dresser is made of light-colored wood with a smooth surface.
   - The white table has a light-colored, possibly glass or laminate surface, with sunlight creating a dotted shadow pattern.
   - The green curtains are partially drawn, allowing sunlight to stream in and create the patterned shadows.

The task requires the agent to move both sets of keys from the dresser to the white table. The agent must first navigate to the dresser, pick up each set of keys one at a time, and then move to the white table to place them down. Since the keys are not inside a container, no opening or closing actions are required.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem move_keys)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        dresser - receptacle
        white_table - receptacle
        keys1 - object
        keys2 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 dresser))
        (not (atLocation agent1 white_table))
        (atLocation keys1 dresser)
        (atLocation keys2 dresser)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 keys1))
        (not (holds agent1 keys2))
        (not (inReceptacle keys1 white_table))
        (not (inReceptacle keys2 white_table))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle keys1 white_table)
        (inReceptacle keys2 white_table)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem move_keys)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        dresser - receptacle
        white_table - receptacle
        keys1 - object
        keys2 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 dresser))
        (not (atLocation agent1 white_table))
        (atLocation keys1 dresser)
        (atLocation keys2 dresser)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 keys1))
        (not (holds agent1 keys2))
        (not (inReceptacle keys1 white_table))
        (not (inReceptacle keys2 white_table))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle keys1 white_table)
        (inReceptacle keys2 white_table)
    ))
)

        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Move two sets of keys from the dresser to the white table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images depict a room with two distinct tables and various objects placed upon them. The task is to move two sets of keys from the dresser to the white table.

**Objects and Their Types:**
- **Receptacles**: There are two tables that function as receptacles for holding objects.
    - A **white table** with a light-colored, possibly glass or laminate top, supported by light wooden legs. It is positioned in the foreground.
    - A **wooden dresser** (or side table) with a natural wood finish, located near a window in the background. This is the source location for the keys.
- **Objects**: The primary objects of interest are the keys and other items on the tables.
    - **Two sets of keys**: These are small, flat, rectangular objects. One set is blue and is located on the wooden dresser. The second set is not clearly visible in any of the provided images, but its existence is implied by the instruction. For the purpose of this description, we will assume it is also on the dresser, possibly obscured or out of frame.
    - Other objects on the white table include a digital alarm clock (brown with a green display showing "8:25"), a smartphone (black screen, white casing), a yellow pencil, and a CD or DVD (iridescent). These are not relevant to the key-moving task.
    - On the wooden dresser, besides the blue keys, there is a smartphone (white casing) and a small, square, black-and-white checkered object (possibly a coaster or a small board).

**Quantities:**
- **Receptacles**: 2 (the white table and the wooden dresser).
- **Sets of keys**: 2 (one blue set is visible on the dresser; the second set is assumed to be present on the dresser as per the instruction).

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The **blue set of keys** is currently **on** the wooden dresser.
- The **second set of keys** is assumed to be **on** the wooden dresser, though not visible in the provided frames.
- The **white table** is a separate receptacle located in a different area of the room, likely closer to the viewer's perspective.
- The robot is not currently at either the dresser or the white table, as per the initial state description.

**Current State Relevant to the Task:**
- The keys are not yet moved. They are in their initial state, resting on the dresser.
- The white table is empty of keys and ready to receive them.
- No actions (like `holds`, `inReceptacle`, `isToggled`, etc.) have been performed yet.

**Visual Properties:**

- The **wooden dresser** has a light wood grain finish and is positioned near a window with dark green curtains.

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
